formula_note: Allocate roughly 58-84% of O'Ballance's whole-Golan-front Israeli dead to 6-9 Oct because Kichen treats the opening as a distinct four-day decisive fight and marks 9 Oct as the turn.
uncertainty_note: Wide interval because no day-by-day Israeli Golan KIA series was found. Best estimate is publishable only as a military numerator, not as a full DR component.
quality_note: Reasonable phase allocation, but still model-generated.

formula_note: Start from O'Ballance's floor of >3,500 Syrian dead on the Golan front and allocate roughly 50-97% to 6-9 Oct, reflecting the opening breakthrough attempt and the four-day turning-point framing.
uncertainty_note: The source gives only a floor for whole-front Syrian dead, so the opening-window range is especially uncertain and should not be over-read.
quality_note: Good strategic fit to the opening phase but numerically weak because the whole-front claim is itself a floor.

formula_note: Sum of side-specific opening-window military-death ranges.
uncertainty_note: Aggregate military numerator is much stronger than the civilian numerator, which remains too sparse for DR publication.
quality_note: Usable as a military numerator only.

formula_note: No auditable front-wide civilian death series located for the opening Golan window; only qualitative injury context was found.
uncertainty_note: Non-publishable for DR. Absence of a clean civilian death registry is the main blocker.

formula_note: No separate unknown-status death series located.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status deaths are excluded rather than silently allocated.

formula_note: Average exposed troops over 4 days: low=(7,000 Israeli + 35,000 Syrian)*4/30; best=(9,500 Israeli + 50,000 Syrian)*4/30; high=(12,000 Israeli + 73,000 Syrian)*4/30.
uncertainty_note: Israeli average force after the first hours is modeled above the opening 7,000 because reserves were arriving during the window.
quality_note: Better grounded than civilian exposure, though still reliant on average-force assumptions.

formula_note: Resident civilian exposure on the occupied plateau over 4 days: 6,400-6,500 residents multiplied by 4/30 month.
uncertainty_note: Conservative footprint excludes broader northern-Israel communities because a stable front-wide civilian polygon/count was not auditable.

formula_note: Low bound is O'Ballance's direct floor (>250 killed in the first 3 canal-front days). Best/high allocate roughly 45-56% of Israel's non-Golan war dead (2,656 total minus 772 Golan-front dead) to the opening Sinai window, consistent with severe losses already visible by 9 October.
uncertainty_note: Very wide interval. The low bound is a firm floor; best/high are model-generated and should not be mistaken for a documentary count.
quality_note: Best available opening-Sinai military numerator, but still partly model-based.

formula_note: Low bound takes Gawrych's direct floor of 280 killed by dawn 7 Oct. Best/high extend that floor through the remainder of the 6-9 Oct opening window while preserving the source's emphasis on initially low Egyptian losses.
uncertainty_note: The source-grounded floor is solid, but full opening-window Egyptian deaths remain uncertain.
quality_note: Conservative because the direct source emphasizes minimal early Egyptian losses.

formula_note: Sum of side-specific opening-window military-death ranges.
uncertainty_note: Military deaths dominate the episode numerically, but the civilian side remains too poorly measured for DR publication.

formula_note: No source-grounded direct civilian-death series located for the canal bridgehead battle strip in 6-9 October.
uncertainty_note: Non-publishable for DR. Civilian deaths may have occurred elsewhere on the front, but they do not map cleanly to this sub-theater/window.

formula_note: No separate unknown-status series located for this episode.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status cases are left unallocated rather than folded into military or civilian totals.

formula_note: Average exposed troops over 4 days: low=(18,000 Israeli + 50,000 Egyptian)*4/30; best=(22,000 Israeli + 75,000 Egyptian)*4/30; high=(30,000 Israeli + 100,000 Egyptian)*4/30.
uncertainty_note: Israeli averages above 18,000 are modeled to reflect reserve arrivals; Egyptian averages span east-bank bridgehead strength to total assault-force scale.
quality_note: Reasonable force-exposure range for the opening canal battle.

formula_note: Civilian denominator withheld because available evidence establishes sparsity but not an auditable sub-theater civilian count for the canal battle strip.
uncertainty_note: Non-publishable. Using whole-Sinai population or even whole-peninsula Bedouin totals would violate the project rules.
